methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $150M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Fourth Power, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Asegun Henry, carries a current post-money valuation of $150M against $39M raised to date. The valuation is benchmarked against a peer universe of 9 private and 1 public comparable (LNZA), with three direct same-stage, same-sector peers examined: Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two valuation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $150M last post-money and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to subsequent stage, implies $300M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $200M, with a range of $110M to $1.8B, reflecting material dispersion in the peer set. The wide gap between Watershed ($1.8B) and Steady Energy ($110M) introduces meaningful uncertainty in relative positioning.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $170M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $250M. The current $150M valuation sits below the low end, suggesting upside to range midpoint of approximately 67% under the triangulated median. Key uncertainties include the peer set composition—particularly the outsized valuation of Watershed relative to other comparables—and the applicability of standard stage-progression multiples in a sector where outcome variance remains elevated.
